title: macOS HIG compliance: semantic colors, reduce-motion, Dynamic Type
source: Apple Human Interface Guidelines for macOS — semantic/system colors, Reduce Motion & Increase Contrast accessibility settings, Dynamic Type (knowledge through Jan 2026)
description: A polished native macOS feel for the Rick Terminal means respecting system appearance and accessibility settings rather than hardcoding the Rick green-on-black theme. Using semantic system colors (so light/dark and Increase Contrast work), honoring Reduce Motion for the portal/animation flourishes, and supporting Dynamic Type makes the app feel native and usable for everyone while preserving the themed accent where appropriate.
proposed_changes: In the SwiftUI app, replace hardcoded Color(hex:) values with semantic colors (Color.primary, .secondary, asset-catalog colors with light/dark variants) and gate the Rick accent behind an asset color that adapts to Increase Contrast. Read @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) to disable or simplify portal/spinner animations. Use relative/Dynamic-Type-aware fonts (e.g. .body, .monospaced) instead of fixed point sizes for log text, and add a native toolbar/menu-bar-extra for sprint controls following macOS HIG.
reject_reason: No files were actually modified (false success)
